legislation: Rules of the High Court, 2011 (Statutory Instrument No. 1 of 2011) — Order 74
notes: Fixed amounts for Section C could not be confirmed from PDF source — marked as null pending verification with practitioner. Section B1(a) max of P2 appears to be a likely printing error in the Gazette — marked for verification. All 'time_based' items use hourly rates from Section I.

hourly_rates.description: Party and Party Scale — hourly rates by years of experience
hourly_rates.note: These are MAXIMUM recoverable rates on a party-and-party basis. Attorney-and-client rates are agreed separately between attorney and client.

items.description: To institute or defend any proceeding
For advice on evidence or on commission
For case on opinion, or for advocate's guidance in preparing pleadings including exceptions
For statement of witness
To set down cause, issue subpoena or any other simple instructions
To draft a petition or affidavit
To note an appeal
To prosecute or defend an appeal (exclusive of perusal of the record)

sections.label: Attendance and Perusal
sections.notes: Fee is per folio (300 words). B1a max of P2 appears to be a printing error in the Gazette — flag for verification with practitioner.

items.description: Attending receipt, entry, perusing and considering: any summons, petition, affidavit, pleading, advocate's advice and drafts, report, and important notice or document
items.max_amount_note: Max of P2 is likely a printing error — verify with practitioner. SA equivalent is P25,000.

items.description: Attending receipt, entry, perusing and considering: any letter, record, stock sheets in voluntary surrenders, judgments or any other material document not elsewhere specified
Attending receipt and considering any plan, exhibit or other material document where folio basis cannot be applied
Making searches in offices of record
Sorting out, arranging and paginating papers for pleading, advice on evidence or brief on trial or appeal
Attending to give or take discovery
Attending on client to obtain particulars of his claim and to settle same
Attending to bespeak and thereafter to procure transcription of shorthand note etc.
Other attendances including telephone calls

sections.label: Attendance (Formal)
sections.notes: Fixed amounts not confirmed from PDF source — amounts marked null pending verification with practitioner.

items.description: To serve or deliver any necessary document, letter or despatch any telegram
To sue out any process or file any document
To set down causes for trial
To search for any return
On receipt, perusing and considering of notice of intention to defend
On advocate (e.g. with folio)
On signature of powers of attorney to sue or defend
Other formal attendances including telephone calls

sections.label: Drafting and Drawing
sections.notes: Charges in this section do not include making the first fair copy, except for items D5, D7, D8 and D10 — copy charges fall under Section F.

items.description: Drafting instructions for case on opinion, for advocate's guidance in preparing pleadings (including further particulars, requests and exceptions)
Drafting instructions to advocate for advice on evidence, for brief on trial or on commission
Drafting instructions to advocate for argument in respect of all classes of pleading (fee for motion, petition, exception or appeal at taxing master's discretion)
Drafting statements of witnesses
Drawing subpoenas, powers of attorney to sue or defend, and formal notices
Drafting a petition, affidavit, any notice (except formal notice), summons, further particulars, writs of execution/arrest/attachment, or any other important document not otherwise provided for
Letter or telegram — first folio
Letter or telegram — subsequent folios
Copy of letter to keep
Drawing index to brief
Drawing short brief
Research, preparation and drafting of heads of argument
Attendance by attorney when advocate is employed — to note judgment only
Attendance by attorney when advocate is employed in court, before a judge, commissioner or referee, or at an inspection — otherwise (not just noting judgment)
Appearance by attorney without an advocate before a judge on request, or before a commissioner or referee
Appearance by attorney as counsel in court, before a judge, arbitrator, commissioner or referee, or at an inspection — maximum as for advocates
items.note: Attorney cannot claim both attorney fees and counsel fees for the same service.

items.description: Conference or consultation with advocate (with or without witnesses) on pleadings, exceptions, particulars, applications, petitions, affidavits, testimony or any other matter the taxing officer deems necessary
Conference or consultation with client, witness or opposite party, or any other conference the taxing officer deems necessary
Attending conference in terms of Order 42 (case management conference)
Any inspection in situ or otherwise
Evidence: just and reasonable charges for procuring evidence and attendance of witnesses allowed on taxation (qualifying expenses require court order or consent of parties)

Briefing and copying: making copies for the court, for counsel, for attorney, for service or any other necessary purpose — per folio (includes first copy of any document drafted under D2, D3, D4, D5, D7 and D9)
Making copies of record in a civil appeal from magistrates' courts — per folio of first copy, and per folio of all other copies
Drawing up insolvency schedules including petition, affidavits and relative attendance ad jurat
For giving a verbal or written opinion (as between attorney and client)
General: inclusive fee for consultations and discussions with client or advocate not otherwise provided or specially charged

sections.label: Bill of Costs
sections.description: Fee for drawing the bill of costs itself — calculated as a percentage of total attorney fees.

items.description: For drawing the bill of costs, making necessary copies and attending settlement
If recourse is had to taxation: for arranging and attending taxation and obtaining consents to taxation
items.note: Only applicable if the bill is formally taxed before the Taxing Master.

items.description: In an undefended civil cause, where the judge does not otherwise direct — attorney may recover without tendering a bill for taxation
items.note: The opposing party may still apply to have the bill taxed even if this nominal amount is claimed.
